title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Mexican Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Rich, flavorful, and perfect for dinner tonight!
longTailKeywords: Chicken Mole recipe step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Mexican Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic Mexican Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) served with rice and tortillas, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, complex flavors of authentic Mexican Chicken Mole, a traditional dish known as Pollo en Mole, perfect for your next family meal.

description: Pollo en Mole is a culinary masterpiece, a rich and complex Mexican dish featuring tender chicken bathed in a deep, dark, and intensely flavorful mole sauce. This traditional recipe balances a symphony of ingredients, from various chiles and spices to nuts, seeds, and a hint of chocolate, creating an unforgettable gastronomic experience.

instructions.title: Prepare and Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Season chicken pieces generously with salt. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken on all sides until golden, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside. Reduce heat to medium.

instructions.title: Toast Chiles and Dry Ingredients
instructions.text: Quickly toast the dried ancho, mulato, and pasilla chiles in the dry pot for 30 seconds per side until fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Remove and rehydrate in 2 cups of hot chicken broth for 15 minutes. In the same pot, add almonds, sesame seeds, pumpkin seeds, cinnamon stick, and whole cloves. Toast until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Tortillas
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the pot. Sauté the chopped onion and garlic until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add the quartered Roma tomatoes and cook until slightly tender, about 5 minutes. Add the corn tortillas and lightly toast them for 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Drain the rehydrated chiles, reserving the soaking liquid. In a blender, combine the rehydrated chiles, sautéed aromatics and tortillas, toasted nuts and spices, ground cumin, ground coriander, and Mexican chocolate. Add 1 cup of the reserved chile soaking liquid and blend until a smooth paste forms. Add more soaking liquid or chicken broth if needed to achieve a thick, smooth consistency.

instructions.title: Simmer the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Pour the mole paste through a fine-mesh sieve into the pot to remove any remaining solids, pressing with a spatula. Discard solids. Bring the mole sauce to a simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly to prevent sticking. Add 1 cup of hot chicken broth and continue to simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, until the sauce thickens and the flavors meld beautifully. Season with salt and sugar to taste.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Mole
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the simmering mole sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged. Cover the pot and continue to simmer on low heat for another 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the chicken is tender and fully coated in the rich mole sauce, it's ready to serve. Garnish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds. Serve hot with traditional Mexican rice, warm corn tortillas, or refried beans.

tips: Toasting chiles and nuts properly is crucial for a deep, complex flavor. Watch them carefully to avoid burning, which can make the sauce bitter.
For a richer mole, use a combination of different chiles. If you prefer a milder mole, use fewer or milder chiles, or remove more of the seeds.
Mole sauce improves with age. Consider making the sauce a day ahead and gently reheating it before adding the cooked chicken for a more profound flavor.
Always pass the blended mole through a fine-mesh sieve. This step ensures a silky-smooth sauce, a hallmark of authentic mole.
article: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce is a beloved Mexican Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
